name: Node 5 — GR86 in-car Pi (Pi 5 2GB, headless OBD hub)
description: Pi OS Lite 64-bit baked into the fleet's Node 5: a HEADLESS in-car Pi 5 (2GB) for the GR86. No HDMI/X/chromium/display stack — the dashboard is viewed via a URL on the iPad/phone. Runs the garage-pi-bridge OBD logger (OBDLink MX+ over the Ugreen BT USB dongle -> Supabase), hosts its own WiFi AP (Sinsera-GR86) on the 2nd radio so the iPad connects DIRECTLY and the local HUD works offline, and uplinks over known WiFi in priority home > iPhone-hotspot > car. Tailscale for headless remote SSH/mgmt (authkey added post-boot). Store-and-forward buffers OBD locally and rsyncs to Node 3's NVMe when home (Node-4 archive pattern). Fleet-integrated via node-status-reporter + node-command-runner (shows on the Nodes page, reboot/poweroff from Kiosks). peta SSH-key-only user + fleet key baked.

preconfigured.user: peta (SSH-key only, sudo NOPASSWD) + fleet key baked
preconfigured.display: NONE — fully headless (gpu_mem=16); HUD is served to the iPad over the Pi AP / cloud
preconfigured.radios: onboard WiFi + TP-Link AX1300 USB (2 WiFi); onboard BT + Ugreen BT 6.0 USB (2 BT). One WiFi hosts the AP, the other uplinks; Ugreen BT -> OBDLink MX+
preconfigured.ap: Sinsera-GR86 (WPA2, method=shared 192.168.42.1/24) — iPad connects direct; passphrase is a placeholder
preconfigured.uplink: NetworkManager autoconnect priority: home-wifi(100) > iphone-hotspot(50) > car-wifi(20); SSIDs/psks are placeholders
preconfigured.obd: garage-obd-bridge.service (Restart=always) at /opt/garage-pi-bridge; .env baked with anon key + owner email, OBDLINK_MAC + CAR_ID + password BLANK placeholders
preconfigured.hud: gr86-hud.service — local-first HUD stub on :8080 (redirects to sinsera.co/garage/<car>/obd?hud=1 when online, placeholder page offline)
preconfigured.tailscale: installed on first boot; NO authkey baked — run `sudo tailscale up --ssh --hostname node5` post-boot
preconfigured.store_forward: gr86-store-forward.timer (5 min) rsyncs /var/lib/garage-obd/buffer -> peta@192.168.4.182:/opt/garage-obd/node5 when Node 3 is reachable (home)
preconfigured.fleet: node-status-reporter + node-command-runner (Supabase node_status / node_commands) — shows as node5 on the Nodes page; reboot/poweroff from Kiosks
preconfigured.run_mode: gr86-idle-shutdown.timer STUB — on with ignition, ~15-30 min parked window then poweroff (needs supercap/UPS-hat wiring)
preconfigured.sd_guard: clean-shutdown-on-power-loss stub + documented overlayfs read-only-root toggle (raspi-config nonint enable_overlayfs) left OFF (conflicts with the OBD buffer)

notes: UNVERIFIED ON HARDWARE — the Pi hasn't arrived. Expect on-Pi tuning: USB dongle drivers (TP-Link AX1300 mt76/rtl88xx-class + Ugreen BT), which radio ends up hosting the AP, OBDLink MX+ BLE pairing.

Placeholders the user MUST fill: OBDLINK_MAC + CAR_ID + SUPABASE_PASSWORD in /opt/garage-pi-bridge/.env; AP passphrase + all uplink SSIDs/psks in the .nmconnection files; Tailscale authkey via `tailscale up`.

Known gaps needing the physical Pi: the REAL gauge HUD (currently a stub that proxies the cloud), the supercap/UPS-hat idle-shutdown wiring, and USB-dongle driver verification. A USB SSD for camera/local buffer and the LiveKit camera publish are Phase 2 (not in this bake).
Headless: no display output at all — Node 5 is an OBD/network hub, not a screen.
